title: diplodium microglossum
intro: Diplodium microglossum, also known as the Greenhood Orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it has been used to treat a variety of ailments. The roots and flowers of Diplodium microglossum are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a decoction or infusion, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
